<!-- Generated by a 19-agent viewport-views feasibility workflow, 2026-06-15. Analysis only; no implementation. -->

# Vibemesh-AI — Viewport Views Feasibility (exploded / per-part / slicer)

## 1. Verdict (up front)

| Ask | Feasible? | Recommended lever | Effort | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **#2 Click a part → show only that part** | **Already shipped.** Works end-to-end today. | Pure additive polish (camera-fit + immediate compile + per-chip spinner) | **Small** | **Low** |
| **#1 "All" view = exploded assembly forming the final shape** | **Yes — model-side.** App-side assembly is *not deterministically possible*. | **Model-side prompt contract** (`server/prompt.mjs`) + `explode` Customizer param; app-side *only* for an explode-multiplier on top of N meshes | **Small** (model-side) / **Large** (app-side N-mesh) | **Medium** (adherence) / **High** (refactor) |
| **#3 "Slicer" view = multiple bed-sized plates** | **Yes — app-side.** Needs the N-mesh refactor + a new 2D bin-packer. | New `viewMode='plates'` over N positioned meshes + `packPlates.ts` + per-plate `.3mf` export | **Large** | **Medium** |

### The single recommended architecture

There are **two independent tracks**, and they should not be conflated:

- **Track A (model-side, cheap, ship first):** Ask #1's *scattered assembly* is a **prompt contract gap**, not a render bug. The viewport renders exactly **one STL** authored by the model's `if(part=="all")` block (`Viewport.tsx:291-336`). The 548×76×215 scatter is the model's own layout choice against an under-specified one-line rule (`prompt.mjs:81`). Fix it in the prompt. **Zero render-side cost, works on every engine.**

- **Track B (app-side, expensive, the real architecture):** A new **`viewMode: 'single' | 'exploded' | 'plates'`** in the store plus a **`pieces[]` array of `{name, stl, bbox, transform}`** that renders **N positioned meshes** instead of one. This is the shared foundation for the *app-side explode multiplier* (#1b) and the *slicer plates view* (#3). The tools that read the single `tbox`/`groupRef` must be **gated per-mode**.

**Recommended sequencing:** Track A (#1a prompt) + #2 polish first for immediate value; then the Track B N-mesh refactor; then #3 plates on top. Crucially — **do not pursue an app-side *assembled* view**: per-piece compiles come back centered (`prompt.mjs:80` "roughly centered"), so the app has *no mating transforms to assemble from*. App-side is the right tool for **explode** and **plate-packing**, not for **assembly**.

---

## 2. Per-ask detail

### Ask #2 — Click a part → show only that part — SHIPPED, polish only

**Already works end-to-end:**
- PARTS bar chips (`Viewport.tsx:589-608`) → `onClick={() => setParamValue('part', value)}` (line 599)
- `setParamValue` (`store.ts:616-626`) sets `paramValues.part`, then after a **350ms debounce** runs `compile(code, buildDefines(...))` → passes `-D part="X"` → returns **one STL** of that piece, which the viewport already renders.
- `'all'` is just another option; `isAssemblyPreview` (`Viewport.tsx:174`) relabels the chip to "Assembly preview" and suppresses the over-bed warning + split button (`Viewport.tsx:467-496`).
- **"Back to All" already exists** — the `'all'` chip is always rendered (`Viewport.tsx:601`). No new control needed. (One finding flagged this as a gap; it is not.)

**Three polish items (all additive, no new render path):**
1. **Camera-fit on part switch (highest value).** `fitVersion` only bumps on empty→full (`store.ts:273`, gated on `s.stl === null`), and the "Grew out of view" banner (`Viewport.tsx:168`) fires *only when bounds grow*. So switching from a big `all` preview to a small piece leaves the camera framed on stale bounds with **no recovery hint** — user must press `F`.
2. **Immediate compile on chip click** — bypass the 350ms `paramTimer`. A part switch reads as navigation, not a slider drag. Safe because `openscad.compile` coalesces/supersedes rapid jobs (`client.ts:33-40`).
3. **Per-chip loading affordance** — during the ~100-500ms recompile only the global HUD "Rendering…" dot (`Viewport.tsx:542-543`) shows. Add a busy state on the clicked chip.

**Recommended design:** a single new store action **`selectPart(value)`** that (a) sets the part value, (b) compiles **immediately** (no timer), (c) bumps `fitVersion`. Rewire the PARTS bar `onClick` to it; add the per-chip spinner.

**Critical constraint:** sliders (`RightPanel.tsx:112`) and chips (`Viewport.tsx:599`) funnel through the **same** `setParamValue`, and the codebase deliberately keeps the camera still mid-iteration (`store.ts:271-272` comment). So the fit logic **must live in the new `selectPart` action, never inside `setParamValue`** — otherwise every slider drag yanks the camera.

---

### Ask #1 — Exploded assembly that forms the final shape

This is the crux, and the trade-off between **model-side** and **app-side** is decisive.

#### Why the scatter happens (root cause — verified)
- The viewport **does not position parts.** The `all` view is literally whatever the model's `if(part=="all")` block draws (`Viewport.tsx:291-336` renders one STL). The 548mm scatter is a **model layout choice**.
- The entire spec of the all-view is one under-specified line — `prompt.mjs:81`: *"`part == "all"` shows a compact assembly preview (pieces in assembled positions). The preview may exceed the bed; individual pieces must not."* There is **no worked example, no anti-scatter rule, no explode param, and no self-check item.**
- The one artifact that proves the model *can* write an assembled block (`KIT_EXEMPLAR`, `exemplars.mjs:6`, which stacks `baseplate()` → `body()` → wheels at real axle positions) is **gated behind `context.kit`** (`providers.mjs:325-332`), which comes from `detectKitIntent()` (`store.ts:178-187`). A "left_bracket / tpu_liner" prompt **almost certainly does not trip** that regex (`/\bkit\b|\bbuildable\b|\bmodular\b|\binterlock/` + a few "parts that…" branches). So the failing model saw the bare one-line rule with **no assembled exemplar** and defaulted to scatter.

#### Recommended: Track A (MODEL-SIDE) — the safe, primary fix

A pure `server/prompt.mjs` edit. Zero render-side cost. Flows through the existing single-STL path on every engine. **Three concrete changes:**

1. **Rewrite `prompt.mjs:81`** to mandate true assembled relative positions (mate the joints from the `// JOINTS:` plan comment at `prompt.mjs:84`), **explicitly forbid** side-by-side / scatter in the all-view, and introduce an **`explode` Customizer number** (`explode = 0; // [0:1:40]`) that offsets each piece along its assembly/separation axis (assembled at 0, fanned out above).
2. **Decouple "is multi-part" from "user said kit."** Add a short **inline assembled-dispatch mini-exemplar** (the `if(part=="all"){ translate(...) a(); translate(...) b(); }` shared-transform pattern) directly into the always-on Multi-part section of `prompt.mjs`, so the demonstration is **unconditionally present for every design carrying a `part` enum** — not gated on `detectKitIntent`. This closes the exact gap the bracket/liner case falls into. (`contextText` in `providers.mjs` only receives `{bed, kit}`, not prompt text, so the cleanest fix is an unconditional inline snippet in the always-on prompt rather than widening the kit gate.)
3. **Add a self-check item** (a 9th, after `prompt.mjs:129`) gated on the presence of a `part` enum: *"the `part=="all"` block places every piece in its assembled mating position (joints touching), NOT spread apart; an `explode` parameter fans them along real fit axes."*

**Why model-side wins:**
- **Zero app changes.** Still renders one STL; `explode` rides the existing `-D` re-render path (`store.ts:616-625`) and **auto-becomes a slider** via `params.ts` — no UI code.
- **Bed checks already tolerate it:** the oversized assembled/exploded bbox is exempt from the over-bed warning (`isAssemblyPreview`, `Viewport.tsx:174`) and the auto-split degenerate check (`store.ts:421` calls `degenerateReason(dims, bed, !isMultiPart)` → `checkBed=false`). No false errors.
- **Zero-cost when untouched:** at `explode=0`, `buildDefines` emits no `-D` at all (`params.ts:135` skips values equal to default).

**The one mandatory addition — make it measurable.** Because this is a non-deterministic prompt nudge and the bench is currently **blind to the scatter symptom** (`bench/buildability.mjs` recompiles each piece individually but **never compiles `part="all"`**), bench coverage is **not optional polish — it is the only thing that converts the prompt nudge into a defensible change under the CLAUDE.md ratchet.** Required in-scope work:
- Add a **multi-part bench task** (kit-style prompt expecting a `part` enum, tagged `multiPart:true`). The all-view bbox is **already captured for free** — `run.mjs:339-340` compiles default `part='all'` → `metrics.size` *is* the 548mm symptom — and per-piece sizes already land in `buildability.pieces[].size` (`run.mjs:359-365`). **No new compile passes needed.**
- Compute a **one-directional** scatter metric (like `overSplit`): `scatterRatio = max over axes of (allViewAxis_i / maxPieceAxis_i)`. A tight assembled/exploded view keeps every axis within a small multiple of the largest piece; the scatter blows one axis up (548.5 vs a ~120mm bracket ≈ 4.5×). **Gate a ceiling** on it (`gate.mjs`, mirror the `overSplit` pattern, wide tolerance initially per the live-API convention).
- **Frame it strictly as a scatter regression guard, not a proof of assembly fidelity** — a model stacking all parts at the origin scores compact-but-wrong. (Pair with the advisory vision-judge if true fidelity matters.)

#### Alternative: Track B (APP-SIDE) — exploded only, *not* assembled

**App-side cannot produce a TRUE assembled view.** Verified blocker: per-piece compiles return each piece **centered at origin** (`prompt.mjs:80` "roughly centered"), carrying **no mating transforms**. The relative positions exist *only* inside the model's `all` block — which the app-side premise discards. Recovering them would require either a **new model-emitted JSON transform manifest** (a bigger, riskier contract change than the prose fix) or **heuristic non-overlap packing** (which *is* ask #3, not assembly).

What app-side *can* do cleanly: an **explode slider over precomputed per-piece offset vectors** — a pure scalar multiply on `group.position`, **no recompile**, smooth/instant. But v1 explode can only be **radial/centroid-outward** (the app has no assembly-axis metadata), not true axis-aware explosion. True axis-aware explode requires model cooperation — reinforcing that **model-side is the lever for #1.**

**Verdict on #1:** Ship **Track A (model-side)** as the primary fix. Reserve app-side N-mesh + explode-multiplier for *after* the Track B foundation lands (below), and only as a radial-explode nicety — never as the assembly source.

---

### Ask #3 — Slicer view = multiple bed-sized plates — feasible, large

This is the genuine architectural change, built on the Track B foundation. **~80% of its render-side cost is shared with the N-mesh refactor.**

**Recommended approach:**

1. **Foundation (shared, Track B):** `viewMode='plates'`; a cached `pieces[]` from a **per-piece compile loop** modeled on `exportPlates`/`export3mf` (`store.ts:797-810` / `860-869`) but **collecting STL buffers in memory** instead of downloading. `stlBBox` (`stl.ts:43-62`) per piece for footprints.

2. **Packer — net-new `src/lib/packPlates.ts`** (greenfield; no packer exists in `src/` today). Pure function, no deps:
   - **Shelf / first-fit-decreasing:** sort pieces by `max(w,h)` descending; walk plates, place on first shelf where it fits, else open a shelf, else open a plate.
   - Input: per-piece XY footprints from `stlBBox` + `plate = resolveBed(bedId, customBed)` (`types.ts:113-118`) shrunk by **MARGIN** and **GAP** (state the convention explicitly: GAP between footprints AND footprint-to-edge margin; threeMF uses 10mm between parts but **zero plate margin**, so there is nothing to literally "mirror").
   - Output: `{ plates: [{index, placements:[{name,x,y,rotated}]}], oversized: [{name,bbox,reason:'footprint'|'height'}] }`.
   - **v1 ships WITHOUT rotation** — rotation correctness depends on the export baking a Z-rotation (deferred, see export below).
   - Complexity O(P·S), sub-millisecond for single-digit part counts. **Unit-test offline** against the 4-part fixture + an oversized case + a multi-plate-overflow case.

3. **Scene layout:** instantiate `PrintBed` (`Viewport.tsx:808-843`, currently origin-centered) **once per plate**, each wrapped in `<group position={[ox,oy,0]}>` (cleaner than adding an offset prop — zero change to PrintBed internals). Place each piece's group at `plateOrigin + packedXY`, dropped to z=0 via `-minZ` (mirrors `threeMF.ts:27`).

4. **Oversize handling:** a piece exceeding `bed.x×bed.y` in **both** orientations → **footprint** oversize; `bbox.z > bed.z` → **height** oversize (packing can't fix it). Render both in an **"Unplaceable / needs splitting" tray** (never silently omit — that's a spec violation, cf. `store.ts:815-820`), each with dims + reason, wired to the **existing per-piece "Ask AI to split" prompt** (`Viewport.tsx:486-494`, which already names the piece, its W×H×D, the bed, and mandates the `part` convention). **Note:** do **not** use `degenerateReason`'s bed check (`store.ts:197`) as the oversize predicate — it is AND-logic across all 3 axes and would never fire for a wide-but-short piece. The predicate lives in `packPlates`.

5. **Camera:** feed `CameraFit`/`CaptureRig`/`ViewRig` a **union tbox** spanning all plates (they consume only center/size — verified). Must **bump `fitVersion`** on entering plate view or the camera won't reframe (`CameraFit` keys off `fitVersion`, `Viewport.tsx:790`).

6. **Export mapping — one `.3mf` PER PLATE:** `exportPlates3mf(fileBase)` modeled on `export3mf` (`store.ts:823-880`). Group placements by plate; for each plate call `buildThreeMF(piecesOnPlate, { arrange:false })` (the `arrange:false` branch already exists, `threeMF.ts:30-34`) with the **packer's exact tx/ty baked into the item transform** — translation needs **no `transformStl`**, it goes straight into the 3MF `<item transform>` matrix exactly like `cursorX` does today (`threeMF.ts:25-29`). **Keep `tz = -bbox.minZ`** (drop-to-bed) in the explicit-transform branch. **Reject Option A** (letting each `.3mf` re-run the naive 1D cursorX arrange): it would re-arrange in a pure X-row ignoring bed width and could **overflow a plate the packer proved fits** — contradicting the on-screen guarantee. For >1 plate, **zip** via `zipSync` (already imported, `threeMF.ts:1`) into `${fileBase}-plates.zip`. Reuse the existing per-piece STL buffers — do not recompile. Keep the loud partial-failure path (`store.ts:873-876`).

---

## 3. The shared foundation (Track B — prerequisite for app-side #1b and #3)

All three multi-mesh asks collapse to **one state change**, and doing it once avoids two divergent N-mesh code paths.

**State (`store.ts`):**
- Add `viewMode: 'single' | 'exploded' | 'plates'` (drop `'assembled'` — not app-derivable; see #1).
- Add `pieces: Array<{ name; stl; bbox; transform:{position;rotation} }> | null` beside the existing `stl`/`stlVersion`/`modelDims` (`store.ts:62`).
- Add a `compilePieces()` action that hoists the per-piece loop (currently duplicated at `store.ts:797-810` and `860-869`) into one place, collecting buffers + `stlBBox` each, behind a busy flag mirroring `exportingPlates`.

**Render (`Viewport.tsx`):**
- Keep the single-mesh branch (`291-336`) for `'single'`.
- Add `pieces.map(p => <group position rotation><mesh geometry={memoizedParse(p.stl)}/></group>)` for multi-mesh modes; generalize the `parseStl` memo (`109-117`) to an array memo.
- **`tbox` becomes a UNION** over piece boxes (`Viewport.tsx:128-137`). This is the **strongest structural lever**: `CameraFit` (`352`), `CaptureRig` (`353`), `ViewRig` (`354`), section clip planes (`178-181`), and measure-sphere scale (`344`) **all consume only `tbox`** — so a union tbox feeds **every framing/section/measure tool unchanged in every mode.** Verified.

**What MUST be gated OFF in multi-mesh modes (v1 = read-only inspection modes):**
| Subsystem | Location | Why |
|---|---|---|
| TransformControls move/rotate gizmo | `Viewport.tsx:338-340`, bound to single `groupRef` | Moving one group of N is meaningless |
| `centerOnBed`/`dropToBed`/`commitTransform` | `store.ts:197-209` | Single-mesh transform |
| `exportStlSmart` baked-transform path | `store.ts:883-915` (bakes `meshTransform`) | Meaningless multi-mesh; route export to `export3mf`/`exportPlates`/`exportPlates3mf` |
| `vpPast`/`vpFuture` undo/redo | store + `Viewport.tsx:430-435` | Assumes one mesh |
| `meshTransform` | — | Stays null/ignored when `viewMode!=='single'` |

**Concurrency constraint (load-bearing):** `openscad` client (`client.ts:30-36`) is a **coalescing singleton** — it keeps only the newest queued job, resolving earlier ones with `error:'superseded'`. So `compilePieces()` **must compile sequentially with `await`** (the proven `for (const piece of pieces) { await openscad.compile(...) }` pattern), **not** N concurrent calls. This is an async build step (4 pieces ≈ a few seconds, each 100-700ms) → needs a **loading state**, cached so re-entering a view or dragging explode doesn't re-render.

**Versioning (decide firmly):** **bump `stlVersion` on any piece-set change.** The deselect/clear-measure effect (`Viewport.tsx:119-125`) and `fitVersion` gating (`store.ts:273`) key on it; reusing it is correct since a piece-set swap *is* a geometry change. No parallel id.

**Cache invalidation:** drop `pieces[]`/plate cache on **code or non-part param changes only**. Guard `name !== 'part'` — a part-chip click also goes through `setParamValue('part', value)` and must **not** drop the cache. Cover the other code-mutation entry points: `adoptCode` (`314-332`), `recompile` (`641-659`), `resetParams` (`628-635`).

**Stale guard:** wrap the batch with the `activeId`/`stale()` pattern (`store.ts:243-244`) against mid-batch edits.

**Default `viewMode='single'`** → the entire existing pipeline (and ask #2) is **byte-for-byte untouched, zero-regression.** Multi-mesh is purely additive behind a mode switch.

**Memory caveat:** N simultaneous `BufferGeometry` for big kits (amplified across plates). Fine for single-digit part counts; cap/guard pathological kits.

**HUD:** the PARTS bar (`Viewport.tsx:589-608`) gains **mode chips (Single / Exploded / Plates)** next to the per-part chips. The explode slider reuses the section-slider component (`Viewport.tsx:439-443`).

---

## 4. Ranked, sequenced plan (impact ÷ effort)

| # | Step | Impact | Effort | Risk | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | **#1a — Model-side prompt fix** (assembled all-block + `explode` param + always-on mini-exemplar + self-check item) in `server/prompt.mjs` | **High** (fixes the actual reported symptom) | **Small** | Medium (adherence) | **DO THIS FIRST.** Zero app code. Directly addresses the 548mm scatter. |
| **2** | **#1a bench guard** — multi-part task + `scatterRatio` metric wired into `gate.mjs` | High (makes #1a defensible under the ratchet) | Small–Medium | Low (eval-only) | **Required, not optional** — without it the gate can't detect improvement or regression. Reuses existing all-view bbox capture. |
| **3** | **#2 — Per-part polish** (`selectPart` action: immediate compile + fit + per-chip spinner) | Medium-High (everyday UX) | Small | Low | Pure additive; no new render path. |
| **4** | **Track B foundation** — `viewMode` + `pieces[]` + `compilePieces()` + union `tbox` + tool gating | Enabling (unblocks #1b & #3) | **Large** | Medium-High | The central, wide-but-mechanical refactor. Discipline = gating single-mesh tools off. |
| **5** | **#1b app-side explode** — N-mesh render + radial explode slider over precomputed offsets | Medium (nice-to-have on top of #1a) | Medium (on the foundation) | Medium | Only *after* #4. Radial-only v1; true axis-explode stays model-side. |
| **6** | **#3 — Slicer plates view** — `packPlates.ts` + multi-bed scene + oversize tray + `exportPlates3mf` | Medium-High (distinct slicer mental model) | **Large** | Medium | On the foundation. Packer is net-new; export is `arrange:false` + baked tx/ty, zip multi-plate. |

**What to do first:** Steps **1 + 2 + 3** are independently shippable, low-to-small effort, and deliver the highest impact (fix the reported symptom + everyday UX) **with zero architectural risk.** Only commit to the Track B foundation (step 4) once those land and the slicer/explode views are confirmed as priorities.

---

## 5. Open questions / what we deliberately won't do

**Won't do:**
- **App-side *assembled* view (the `'assembled'` viewMode).** Per-piece compiles are centered at origin (`prompt.mjs:80`) with no mating transforms — the app has nothing to assemble from. Assembly stays model-side. (App-side handles explode-multiplier + plate-packing only.)
- **Per-piece move/measure/section gizmos in multi-mesh modes (v1).** Plate/explode views are **read-only inspection modes.** Retrofitting the gizmo + per-piece transform history would explode scope. Selecting a chip or "All" exits to the live single-STL path.
- **Piece rotation in the packer (v1).** Deferred until export bakes the Z-rotation (`transformStl` + `composeMatrix`, `stl.ts:69` / `store.ts:159`). Contract: the packer must not emit rotated placements until export bakes them, or WYSIWYG silently breaks.
- **A guillotine/maxrects packer.** Shelf-FFD is the right altitude for single-digit part counts; density isn't print-critical for a preview.

**Open questions:**
1. **Did the failing run trip `detectKitIntent`?** The actual prompt is unobservable. The fix is robust either way — even *with* the exemplar reached, the prose under-specification independently permits scatter — but it motivates making the assembled guidance **always-on for any `part` enum** rather than kit-gated.
2. **`scatterRatio` tolerance** on a non-deterministic live API — start wide (CLAUDE.md convention), tighten with `BENCH_SAMPLES` aggregation.
3. **Memory ceiling** for large kits in plate view (N simultaneous BufferGeometries × plates). Need a piece-count cap / LOD threshold.
4. **CaptureRig in multi-mesh modes** would change what the refine loop sees (it currently snapshots the single model). For v1, refine should continue to capture `'single'` — verify the union-tbox capture doesn't silently corrupt the refine loop before enabling it for explode/plates.
5. **Does `'exploded'` need its own export, or does it reuse `export3mf`?** Explode is a *view* of the assembly; export should remain per-piece (`export3mf`/`exportPlates`). Confirm no user expectation of an "exploded .3mf."

---
